content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains a single integer N, the number of distinct integers. The second line contains N space-separated distinct integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of pairs (i, j) such that 1 ≤ i ≤ N, 1 ≤ j ≤ N, and ai < aj.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Case 1: Only one such pair of (i, j) indices exists: (2,1), since a2 < a1. Case 2: 3 possible pairs of indices: (2,1), (2,3), (3,1).
Explanation: There are 6 pairs of indices (i, j) such that ai < aj: (1,2), (1,3), (1,4), (2,3), (2,4), (3,4).
Explanation: There are no pairs of indices (i, j) such that ai < aj.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by sorting the array and then counting the number of pairs (i, j) such that ai < aj. This can be done by iterating over the sorted array and for each element, counting the number of elements to its right that are greater than it.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting, O(T * N log N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for sorting and storing the input array.
